messages.content: hey, so I've been thinking about how much better I feel lately? like I'd honestly rate my wellbeing at 8/10 right now which is wild considering where I was in December
That's a significant improvement! What do you think has contributed most to feeling better?

honestly I think it's the clarity more than anything? like I finally told Reeves about the Google offer last week and it was awkward as hell but went okay. he was definitely disappointed but he agreed to write me a recommendation letter anyway
That must have been a tough conversation, but it sounds like it went better than expected. How did you approach telling him?

I basically just ripped the band-aid off and said I'd accepted an industry position. he got quiet for like 30 seconds which felt like forever, then asked if I was sure about not doing the PhD. when I said yes he just nodded and said he'd support whatever I decided
It sounds like he handled it professionally even if he was disappointed. How are you feeling about the industry decision now?

honestly? really good. like I have this clarity now that industry is the right call for me, at least for now. and I'm back to climbing twice a week again which feels amazing, plus I'm actually sleeping 7 hours most nights instead of the 4-5 I was getting during thesis crunch
The sleep and exercise routine returning is huge for wellbeing. It sounds like having that career decision made has lifted a weight off your shoulders.
exactly! and Jess noticed too, she said I seem way less stressed when I'm around the apartment. it's funny how much mental energy I was spending just on the uncertainty of what comes after graduation
It's great that your roommate has noticed the positive change too. How are you feeling about wrapping up your thesis now that the career path is settled?

way less pressure honestly. like I still want to do good work and defend well in May, but it's not this make-or-break thing anymore where my entire future depends on it. I can just focus on finishing strong without all the anxiety about what it means for my career

summary: Kai reflects on significantly improved wellbeing (8/10) after telling their advisor about accepting Google's offer and gaining clarity about choosing industry over academia. They've returned to regular climbing and better sleep habits.
